repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #77539 so steered or follow-up subagent task text is persisted through replacement runs and replayed correctly by restart/orphan redispatch, while addressing the concrete review-bot comment-reference findings and preserving @amittell's contribution.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(subagent): preserve steered task text on restart redispatch
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
Repair source PR #77539 by @amittell so restart/orphan redispatch preserves the task text actually dispatched after steer, wake, or completed-session follow-up replacement. Keep the patch confined to subagent restart/redispatch behavior and directly related tests.

## Repair scope
- Address the concrete review-bot findings about stale `redispatchSubagentRunAfterRestart` comment references.
- Preserve the task override through replacement run records where the caller supplied steered or follow-up text.
- Keep omitted-task callers backward-compatible by preserving the prior task.

## Validation
- `pnpm -s vitest run src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/gateway/session-subagent-reactivation.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce.format.e2e.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.send-followup-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`
- Codex `/review`

Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77539
Credit: Thanks @amittell for the original fix and runtime proof.
credit_notes: Preserve contributor credit for @amittell as the author of source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77539.
If a replacement PR is opened instead of updating the contributor branch, the PR body must name #77539 as the source PR and credit @amittell.
